hypothesis: In MADE extended with two-fidelity oracles (cheap MLIP and expensive DFT) on held-out ternary systems, an agent that promotes candidates to DFT only when a Bayesian convex-hull–aware posterior probability of DFT stability exceeds 0.8 will achieve the same number of DFT-validated novel stable compounds as a baseline that promotes all MLIP-stable candidates while using at least 35% fewer DFT evaluations under a total budget of 200 MLIP queries and 40 DFT slots.
